plan.title: Plan - Segmented Streaming Platform Alignment
plan.objective: Create an execution-ready, governance-compliant plan that aligns segmented DASH startup/recovery with mature platform patterns while respecting our no-CDN and no-direct-fallback architecture constraints.
in_scope: Define deterministic segmented startup contract across session create, manifest readiness, and first segment fetch failure paths.
Machine-readable startup hint response contract plus retry header behavior implemented and validated in backend route tests.
out_of_scope: Do not redesign product scope beyond segmented playback reliability; do not add direct stream fallback paths; do not include YouTube-specific, infra-only, or unrelated recommendation feature work in this track.

tasks.summary: Week 1 Day 1-2: Draft and agree segmented startup contract document and route-level response taxonomy.
acceptance_criteria: Contract doc captures ready, transient, and terminal startup states.
Route handlers and service layer mapping are identified line-by-line.
Operator-facing retry semantics are explicit and testable.
tasks.outcome: Added ADR documenting segmented startup contract semantics and route-level response taxonomy for session create, manifest, and segment readiness paths. | Documented startup correlation header expectations and observability implications for rollout diagnostics.
evidence: verify: manual review of docs/architecture/adr-segmented-streaming.md against backend startupHint implementation in backend/src/routes/streaming.ts

tasks.summary: Week 1 Day 2-3: Implement startup response taxonomy and retry hints in backend segmented routes/services.
acceptance_criteria: Transient startup responses are machine-readable and stable.
Session create/manifest readiness states are bounded and deterministic.
Existing segmented token/session security guarantees remain intact.
tasks.outcome: Added structured startupHint contract and Retry-After header behavior for segmented startup failures on session-create/manifest/segment routes. | Added runtime route tests covering transient/terminal startup hint responses and header behavior.

tasks.summary: Week 1 Day 3-4: Add origin-shield microcache and request-coalescing for manifest/first segments.
acceptance_criteria: Duplicate startup requests collapse to single backend fill operations.
Cache behavior for startup assets is observable via logs/metrics.
No cache policy breaks segmented authorization checks.
tasks.outcome: Implemented in-flight request coalescing for manifest readiness and segment readiness waits keyed by session and session+segment. | Added focused service tests proving coalescing behavior and cleanup after failure. | Added short-TTL readiness microcache for manifest and segment startup checks to avoid duplicate timeline/file reads during bursty startup retries. | Added segmented trace events for readiness microcache hits and coalesced waits to make cache behavior observable during rollout debugging.

tasks.summary: Week 1 Day 4-5: Reduce player startup to staged deterministic recovery and remove incidental complexity.
acceptance_criteria: Recovery stages are bounded and policy-driven.
RECOVERING/LOADING transitions remain coherent and test-covered.
No direct fallback behavior exists in startup failure paths.
tasks.outcome: Aligned segmented startup retry scheduling with backend startupHint semantics (transient + retryAfterMs) while keeping recovery stage decisions policy-driven and bounded. | Verified startup timeout path schedules segmented staged retries and transitions through RECOVERING/LOADING until retry budget exhaustion, then surfaces ERROR without direct fallback. | Added explicit terminal stage-exhaustion policy coverage to guarantee startup retry flows stop in ERROR when retry/session-reset budget is spent instead of falling back to direct playback. | Added startup-stability eligibility guard for segmented handoff recovery so early playback errors no longer trigger handoff churn during the first stabilized playback window.

tasks.summary: Week 2 Day 1-2: Implement CMCD/CMSD-aligned startup telemetry dimensions and server correlation fields.
acceptance_criteria: Client metrics include stage, retry budget, and startup latency dimensions.
Server logs/metrics correlate session/create/manifest/segment startup timings.
Telemetry allows baseline-vs-wave comparisons without ad hoc parsing.
tasks.outcome: Added CMCD/CMSD-aligned startup dimensions to player startup timeline telemetry, including retry budgets and session reset budget usage. | Added startup correlation headers/fields across segmented session-create, manifest, and segment route metrics and trace payloads. | Extended startup metric ingestion on backend client-metrics route for correlation and retry-budget dimensions.

tasks.summary: Week 2 Day 2-3: Add targeted regression suites for startup contract, staged recovery, and retry exhaustion behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Backend tests cover startup readiness and transient/terminal response codes.
Frontend tests cover staged recovery decisions and state transitions.
Tests prove segmented-only policy under retry exhaustion.
tasks.outcome: Expanded route-level runtime tests to validate startup contract fields, correlation headers, and transient/terminal startup behavior. | Validated staged startup recovery and retry exhaustion behavior via targeted frontend policy/unit test suites. | Validated startup hint parser + conservative delay behavior in dedicated helper tests.

tasks.summary: Week 2 Day 3-4: Run rollout-wave validation and compare startup SLO-like metrics against baseline.
acceptance_criteria: Wave report includes first-audible, startup timeout, and retry-exhaustion rates.
Baseline comparison is documented with explicit deltas and confidence caveats.
Residual risks and follow-up optimizations are ranked by impact.
tasks.outcome: Added baseline report generator for segmented startup metrics (p50/p95 first-audible latency, startup-timeout rate, retry-exhaustion rate) from NDJSON captures. | Added architecture docs and sample capture for baseline vs wave analysis workflow. | Added baseline-vs-wave comparison mode to startup baseline measurement script with absolute and percent deltas for p50/p95 latency, startup-timeout rate, and retry-exhaustion rate. | Added explicit confidence caveat output based on startup sample count and documented residual-risk prioritization for rollout triage. | Execution halted on 2026-03-02T12:51:53.265Z due to plan archival closure.
evidence: verify: cd backend && npx tsx scripts/measure-segmented-startup-baseline.ts --input ../docs/architecture/segmented-streaming-baseline-sample.ndjson --label sample
verify: cd backend && npx tsx scripts/measure-segmented-startup-baseline.ts --input ../docs/architecture/segmented-streaming-baseline-sample.ndjson --label baseline-sample

verify: cd backend && npx tsx scripts/measure-segmented-startup-baseline.ts --input ../docs/architecture/segmented-streaming-baseline-sample.ndjson --label baseline-sample --compare-input ../docs/architecture/segmented-streaming-baseline-sample.ndjson --compare-label wave-sample

tasks.summary: Week 2 Day 5: Document operator playbook, rollout guardrails, and closeout evidence in canonical docs.
acceptance_criteria: Deployment docs include startup contract and retry behavior expectations.
Observability docs include query examples for startup regression detection.
Changelog entry summarizes user-visible reliability outcomes.
tasks.outcome: Documented segmented startup contract fields, correlation headers, and log query dimensions for rollout diagnostics in deployment docs. | Expanded deployment playbook with startup contract semantics, startup correlation headers, observability field groups, and baseline-vs-wave measurement commands. | Updated architecture guidance to use automated baseline-vs-wave comparison reporting instead of manual markdown diffing. | Recorded reliability-facing rollout updates in changelog for operator/user visibility.
evidence: verify: manual review of docs/DEPLOYMENT.md startup contract and observability sections
verify: manual review of docs/DEPLOYMENT.md startup contract + observability + compare command sections
verify: manual review of docs/architecture/segmented-streaming-baseline.md runbook and residual risk ranking

tasks.summary: Remediation P0-1: Implement atomic force-regenerate staging/promote flow for DASH assets (build in staging dir, validate, atomic swap).
acceptance_criteria: forceRegenerateDashSegments writes to an isolated staging directory and only promotes artifacts via atomic rename after manifest and startup assets validate.
No partially regenerated asset directory is externally visible during regeneration; existing stable assets remain serveable until swap.
Regression coverage proves interrupted regeneration leaves prior assets intact and subsequent regenerate succeeds cleanly.
tasks.outcome: Refactored force-regenerate flow to build DASH outputs in an isolated staging directory, validate manifest/startup assets, and promote via atomic directory swap. | Added failure-safe cleanup so failed or interrupted regeneration leaves previously promoted assets intact and still serveable.

tasks.summary: Remediation P0-2: Fix ensureLocalDashSegments failure/inFlight race ordering so concurrent callers receive deterministic outcomes.
acceptance_criteria: Concurrent ensureLocalDashSegments calls share one in-flight build contract and do not expose stale/optimistic paths before readiness semantics are met.
Failure propagation and inFlightBuilds cleanup ordering are deterministic so retries do not inherit stale failure state or orphaned promises.
Concurrency-focused tests cover success, failure, and post-failure retry behavior under parallel callers.
tasks.outcome: Reordered ensureLocalDashSegments in-flight/failure handling so concurrent callers share a single deterministic build promise and never observe optimistic readiness before validation. | Normalized inFlightBuilds cleanup and retry ordering after failures so retries do not inherit stale failure state or orphaned promises.

tasks.summary: Remediation P1-1: Eliminate handoff listener orphans with explicit track-change/unmount cleanup plus timeout backstop.
acceptance_criteria: Handoff load/loaderror listeners are tracked and cleared on track-change transitions and component unmount.
A bounded timeout backstop clears orphan listeners and buffering state when expected callback events never arrive.
Regression tests demonstrate listener count remains bounded across repeated handoff attempts and rapid track changes.
tasks.outcome: Added explicit handoff listener lifecycle tracking and teardown across track-change transitions and component unmount. | Added a timeout backstop that clears stalled handoff listeners and buffering state when load callbacks never arrive.
evidence: verify: npm --prefix frontend run lint -- components/player/AudioPlaybackOrchestrator.tsx lib/audio-engine/segmentedPlaybackRegressionPolicy.ts tests/unit/segmentedPlaybackRegressionPolicy.test.ts lib/listen-together-socket.ts tests/component/listenTogetherSocketRetry.component.test.ts

tasks.summary: Remediation P1-2: Add heartbeat consecutive-failure guarded refresh path to avoid repeated churn on transient heartbeat outages.
acceptance_criteria: Client heartbeat logic tracks consecutive failures and resets streak state on success.
After configured streak threshold, a guarded single-flight refresh/recovery path runs with cooldown protection.
Heartbeat metrics/log fields record failure streak and guarded refresh outcome for observability and alert tuning.
tasks.outcome: Implemented heartbeat consecutive-failure streak tracking with reset-on-success semantics. | Added threshold-guarded single-flight refresh with cooldown protection to prevent repeated churn during transient heartbeat outages, including observable outcome fields.
evidence: verify: npm --prefix frontend run lint -- components/player/AudioPlaybackOrchestrator.tsx lib/audio-engine/segmentedPlaybackRegressionPolicy.ts tests/unit/segmentedPlaybackRegressionPolicy.test.ts lib/listen-together-socket.ts tests/component/listenTogetherSocketRetry.component.test.ts

tasks.summary: Remediation P1-3: Reclassify startup fallback semantics finding as audit/observability check (false-positive) rather than behavior flip.
acceptance_criteria: Startup fallback behavior remains unchanged unless audit evidence demonstrates a reproducible product-impacting fault.
Instrumentation captures fallback trigger reason, parsed startup hint details, and retry-hint provenance for post-incident analysis.
Audit disposition is documented as false-positive with explicit follow-up observability checks and close criteria.

tasks.summary: Remediation P2-1: Introduce bounded readiness polling backoff strategy for asset/startup readiness checks (not a pure fs.watch swap).
acceptance_criteria: waitForAssetFile and waitForStartupWindowReady move from fixed-interval polling to bounded backoff with jitter and explicit maximum interval cap.
Backoff resets promptly when build progress/readiness signals are observed, preserving fast path startup latency.
Tests cover backoff progression, reset behavior, and timeout boundary semantics.

tasks.summary: Remediation P2-2: Add periodic sweeping for segmented in-memory maps to cap stale entry growth.
acceptance_criteria: Expired entries in inMemorySessions/readiness microcache maps are swept on a bounded cadence without affecting active sessions.
Sweep logic is deterministic, low-overhead, and avoids removing entries still referenced by active in-flight work.
Tests validate stale eviction behavior under churn-heavy session create/heartbeat/readiness paths.

tasks.summary: Remediation P2-3: Implement per-user segmented session create limiting that is Redis-aware and distributed-safe across pods.
acceptance_criteria: Session create rate/concurrency limits are enforced through Redis-backed atomic operations so all pods observe the same limit envelope.
Limit rejections return explicit contract-compliant status/code/hint and preserve existing authorization/validation ordering.
Limiter state decays automatically (TTL/windowing) and emits metrics for tuning and incident diagnostics.

tasks.summary: Remediation P2-4: Harden Express sendFile callback/error handling for manifest and segment responses.
acceptance_criteria: Segment and manifest handlers use sendFile callback handling to surface stream errors/aborts without double response writes.
ENOENT and transport errors from sendFile callback map into existing segmented startup error taxonomy and telemetry fields.
Route runtime tests cover callback error/abort branches and preserve success-path response headers/types.

tasks.summary: Remediation P2-5: Harden manifest timeline parsing to tolerate malformed or edge SegmentTimeline patterns safely.
acceptance_criteria: Timeline parsing handles attribute order/whitespace variance and malformed repeat values without unsafe counts or crashes.
When timeline parsing cannot derive safe counts, readiness behavior degrades predictably with explicit trace/metric annotations.
Unit tests add malformed, multi-representation, and large-repeat manifest fixtures.

tasks.summary: Remediation P2-6: Deduplicate LOSSLESS extension regex into shared segmented-streaming constant/helper.
acceptance_criteria: A single shared LOSSLESS extension matcher is consumed by segmentService and sessionService to remove divergence risk.
Duplicate local regex literals are removed with no behavior change in codec/quality decisions.
Tests confirm representative lossless/non-lossless extensions retain prior classification results.

tasks.summary: Remediation P2-7: Add AbortController management for segmented prewarm fetches and superseded validation requests.
acceptance_criteria: Prewarm manifest/segment validation fetches are issued with AbortControllers tied to track/session lifecycle.
Controllers are canceled on track-change, retry supersession, and unmount to prevent orphan network activity.
Telemetry distinguishes intentional aborts from true prewarm validation failures.
tasks.outcome: Added AbortController lifecycle ownership for segmented prewarm validation fetches tied to active track/session context. | Canceled superseded prewarm validations on track change, retry supersession, and unmount, and separated intentional abort reporting from true validation failures.
evidence: verify: npm --prefix frontend run lint -- components/player/AudioPlaybackOrchestrator.tsx lib/audio-engine/segmentedPlaybackRegressionPolicy.ts tests/unit/segmentedPlaybackRegressionPolicy.test.ts lib/listen-together-socket.ts tests/component/listenTogetherSocketRetry.component.test.ts

tasks.summary: Define MP4Box exploration charter, hard success metrics, and explicit no-pivot guardrails.
acceptance_criteria: Success metrics include bad-chunk detection lift, startup-latency impact ceiling, and operational complexity budget.
Guardrails explicitly prohibit replacing FFmpeg transcoding in this exploration phase.
Decision gate and owners are documented with concrete evidence requirements.

tasks.summary: Capture baseline FFmpeg-only reliability/latency evidence for segmented sessions before MP4Box trials.
acceptance_criteria: Baseline includes first-audible latency (p50/p95), playback-error rate, segment-quarantine rate, and forced-regeneration frequency.
Baseline windows and sample-size caveats are recorded for comparability.
Evidence is saved in reproducible artifacts, not ad-hoc terminal output.

tasks.summary: Build an offline validation harness that runs MP4Box checks against FFmpeg-generated DASH outputs and compares results against current validators.
acceptance_criteria: Harness executes deterministically on a track corpus and records pass/fail + reason for MP4Box, ffprobe, and current structural checks.
False-positive and false-negative cases are explicitly identified.
Harness output format supports baseline-vs-wave style comparison.

tasks.summary: Create a known-problem segment corpus (including previously failing chunk windows) and run reproducibility trials.
acceptance_criteria: Corpus includes at least one previously observed fast-fail track/chunk pattern and representative healthy tracks.
Trials record whether MP4Box flags corruption earlier or more accurately than current checks.
All corpus items are traceable to concrete cache/session evidence.

tasks.summary: Benchmark MP4Box validation overhead and operational impact under realistic concurrency.
acceptance_criteria: Measured overhead includes build wall-time delta, CPU, and memory under at least low/medium/high parallelism tiers.
p95 startup latency budget impact is quantified and compared against declared exploration thresholds.
Operational concerns (binary packaging, image size, failure modes) are captured with mitigation notes.

tasks.summary: Produce a feature-flagged integration spike design for optional MP4Box post-build validation (temp-build -> validate -> atomic promote).
acceptance_criteria: Design keeps live playback request path non-blocking and avoids per-segment inline validation.
Failure behavior is explicit: reject/purge bad temp assets and trigger deterministic regeneration path.
Integration plan reuses existing observability and repair signals where possible.

tasks.summary: Define canary evaluation and rollback criteria for optional MP4Box validation.
acceptance_criteria: Canary scope, duration, and guardrails are explicit and reversible.
Go/no-go thresholds include reliability lift, latency impact, and operational incident rate.
Rollback and disable procedures are documented as first-class runbook steps.

tasks.summary: Publish final exploration decision memo: keep FFmpeg-only, adopt MP4Box as validator, or escalate to broader packaging redesign.
acceptance_criteria: Decision cites quantitative evidence from baseline, harness, corpus, and benchmark phases.
Memo includes explicit rationale for rejected alternatives and residual risks.
Follow-up work is split into atomic queue-ready tasks when further action is approved.

spec_outline.summary: Align segmented DASH startup and recovery behavior with mature platform patterns by shifting reliability burden to deterministic server contracts, origin-shield style caching, and minimal player-side recovery logic under constrained non-CDN infrastructure.

full_spec_outline.objective: Define deterministic segmented startup contract across session create, manifest readiness, and first segment fetch failure paths.
full_spec_outline.deliverable: Machine-readable startup hint response contract plus retry header behavior implemented and validated in backend route tests.
acceptance_criteria: Startup-path errors include stable machine-readable hint fields for stage/state/transient/retry guidance.
Transient startup states expose bounded retry hints via response payload and header semantics.

refined_spec.summary: Use a two-week, low-risk execution sequence that first hardens startup contracts and cache behavior, then simplifies player interactions and validates with measurable startup SLO deltas. Preserve segmented-only playback architecture and avoid introducing direct URL fallback paths.

full_refined_spec.decision: Use backend-authored startup transientness and retry-after hints as the primary startup retry signal, with frontend heuristic fallback only when hints are absent.
full_refined_spec.rationale: Server-side classification has canonical knowledge of startup state and reduces brittle message-string retry logic in the player.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend startup retry policy consults parsed backend startup hints before heuristic fallback.
Retry delay logic remains conservative and bounded to existing recovery windows.

implementation_plan.summary:
Three-track execution plan: (A) complete segmented reliability rollout-wave validation, (B) execute the reviewed stabilization remediation backlog in strict priority order (P0 atomic regeneration/race fixes, then P1 handoff-heartbeat-observability controls, then P2 hardening tasks), and (C) continue the bounded MP4Box exploration once stabilization blockers are implemented or explicitly waived.

Archived on 2026-03-02T12:51:53.265Z by user request. Segmented streaming platform alignment plan closed; MP4Box exploration workstream aborted.

steps.deliverable: Remediation P2-3: Implement per-user segmented session create limiting that is Redis-aware and distributed-safe across pods.
acceptance_criteria: Session create rate/concurrency limits are enforced through Redis-backed atomic operations so all pods observe the same limit envelope.
Limit rejections return explicit contract-compliant status/code/hint and preserve existing authorization/validation ordering.
Limiter state decays automatically (TTL/windowing) and emits metrics for tuning and incident diagnostics.

steps.deliverable: Remediation P2-4: Harden Express sendFile callback/error handling for manifest and segment responses.
acceptance_criteria: Segment and manifest handlers use sendFile callback handling to surface stream errors/aborts without double response writes.
ENOENT and transport errors from sendFile callback map into existing segmented startup error taxonomy and telemetry fields.
Route runtime tests cover callback error/abort branches and preserve success-path response headers/types.

steps.deliverable: Remediation P2-5: Harden manifest timeline parsing to tolerate malformed or edge SegmentTimeline patterns safely.
acceptance_criteria: Timeline parsing handles attribute order/whitespace variance and malformed repeat values without unsafe counts or crashes.
When timeline parsing cannot derive safe counts, readiness behavior degrades predictably with explicit trace/metric annotations.
Unit tests add malformed, multi-representation, and large-repeat manifest fixtures.

steps.deliverable: Remediation P2-6: Deduplicate LOSSLESS extension regex into shared segmented-streaming constant/helper.
acceptance_criteria: A single shared LOSSLESS extension matcher is consumed by segmentService and sessionService to remove divergence risk.
Duplicate local regex literals are removed with no behavior change in codec/quality decisions.
Tests confirm representative lossless/non-lossless extensions retain prior classification results.

steps.deliverable: Remediation P2-7: Add AbortController management for segmented prewarm fetches and superseded validation requests.
acceptance_criteria: Prewarm manifest/segment validation fetches are issued with AbortControllers tied to track/session lifecycle.
Controllers are canceled on track-change, retry supersession, and unmount to prevent orphan network activity.
Telemetry distinguishes intentional aborts from true prewarm validation failures.

delivered: Added AbortController lifecycle ownership for segmented prewarm validation fetches tied to active track/session context.
Canceled superseded prewarm validations on track change, retry supersession, and unmount, and separated intentional abort reporting from true validation failures.

pre_spec_outline.purpose_goals: Create an execution-ready, governance-compliant plan that aligns segmented DASH startup/recovery with mature platform patterns while respecting our no-CDN and no-direct-fallback architecture constraints.
pre_spec_outline.non_goals: Do not redesign product scope beyond segmented playback reliability; do not add direct stream fallback paths; do not include YouTube-specific, infra-only, or unrelated recommendation feature work in this track.
